text: The DUAP System shall collect probe vehicle data.
The DUAP data elements shall include probe vehicle data fields corresponding to the SAE J2735 format as enumerated in APPENDIX C.
The DUAP Input Services shall be able to collect probe vehicle data from the VII Proof of Concept Probe Data Service using the X-031 interface.
The DUAP Input Services shall be able to collect probe vehicle data from the Chrysler Fast Feedback fleet using the Chrysler telematics interface.
The DUAP Input Services shall be able to collect probe vehicle data from Southeast Michigan Snow and Ice Management using the SEMSIM interface.
The DUAP Input Services shall be able to collect probe vehicle data from the MDOT fleet using the MDOT probe vehicle data interface.
The DUAP System shall be able to collect weather data.
The DUAP Input Services shall be able to collect weather data from the Road Commission for Oakland County Road Weather Information Systems using the RCOC RWIS interface.
The DUAP Input Services shall be able to collect Clarus weather data as enumerated in APPENDIX D.
The DUAP Input Services shall include weather condition fields corresponding to the SAE J2354 WeatherInformation structure as enumerated in APPENDIX E.
The DUAP System shall collect roadway traffic data.
The DUAP data elements shall include roadway event information data fields corresponding to the SAE J2354 structure as enumerated in APPENDIX F.
The DUAP Input Services shall include roadway traffic data fields corresponding to the SAE J2354 structure as enumerated in APPENDIX G.
The DUAP Input Services shall be able to collect roadway traffic data from the MITS Center using the MITS traffic data interface.
The DUAP Input Services shall be able to collect roadway traffic data from NAVTEQ Corporation's Traffic.com using the Traffic.com interface.
The DUAP System shall be able to collect road condition data.
The DUAP System shall be able to collect work zone (lane closure) data.
The DUAP System shall log Input Services data transactions.
The DUAP System shall be able to organize the sequence of execution of computational modules.
The DUAP System shall allow the system administrator to add modify and delete Input Services.
The DUAP System shall allow the system administrator to add modify and delete Computational Services.
The DUAP System shall allow the system administrator to add modify and delete Output Services.
The DUAP System shall allow the system administrator to add modify and delete Presentation Services.
The DUAP System shall restrict access to authorized users.
The DUAP System shall keep records of user access.
The DUAP System shall allow a system administrator to add modify and delete user access rights.
The DUAP System shall be able to log system operations.
The DUAP System shall log Administrative Services data transactions.
The DUAP System shall cache raw and derived probe vehicle data.
The DUAP System shall cache raw and derived traffic data.
The DUAP System shall cache raw and derived road weather data.
The DUAP System shall cache raw and derived road condition data.
The DUAP System shall cache data only if the record associates the data with its source a geo-referenced location and a timestamp.
The DUAP System shall cache data for 1 week.
The DUAP System shall allow data to be accessed as soon as it is cached.
The DUAP System shall log Dynamic Data Services data transactions.
The DUAP System shall derive traffic data from probe vehicle data.
The DUAP Computational Services shall be able to derive speed from probe vehicle data.
The DUAP Computational Services shall be able to derive congestion level based on volume to capacity from probe vehicle data.
The DUAP Computational Services shall be able to derive freeway queue length from probe vehicle data.
The DUAP Computational Services shall be able to derive travel time from probe vehicle data.
The DUAP System shall be able to infer incidents from probe vehicle data.
The DUAP Computational Services shall be able to infer incident occurrences from probe vehicle data.
The DUAP Computational Services shall be able to infer incident locations from probe vehicle data.
The DUAP System shall be able to derive road surface weather observations from probe vehicle data.
The DUAP Computational Services shall be able to derive pothole location from probe vehicle data.
The DUAP System shall be able to identify high risk locations.
The DUAP System shall be able to calculate measures of effectiveness.
The DUAP System shall be able to perform data quality checking.
The DUAP System shall log Computational Services data transactions.
The DUAP System shall store geo-references for Michigan roadways.
The DUAP System shall store DUAP system configuration data.
The DUAP System shall archive cached data.
The DUAP Persistent Data Services shall be able to archive cached raw and derived probe vehicle data.
The DUAP System shall log Persistent Data Services data transactions.
The DUAP System shall publish data.
The DUAP Output Services shall be able to publish traveler data in SAE J2354 format.
The DUAP Output Services shall be able to publish traffic data to TMS using the TMS interface format.
The DUAP Output Services shall be able to publish weather data elements as enumerated in APPENDIX D.
The DUAP System shall publish alerts.
The DUAP Output Services shall be able to publish alerts using the threshold exceeded alert interface format.
The DUAP System shall log Output Services data transactions.
The DUAP Presentation Services shall present data published by Output Services.
The DUAP Presentation Services shall include a user interface for browsing traffic information.
The traffic information browser shall allow a user to query traffic-related information using a web browser supporting HTML 4.01 JavaScript and XML 1.0.
The information browsers shall provide a map display which uses icons to identify the locations with associated data.
The DUAP Presentation Services shall include a user interface for browsing incident information.
The DUAP Presentation Services shall include a user interface for browsing weather information.
The DUAP System shall require users to log in for system access.
The DUAP System shall log Presentation Services data transactions.
The DUAP System shall conform to IT standards established by the Michigan Department of Information Technology.
The DUAP System shall handle database data requests through standard Structured Query Language commands.
The DUAP System shall use Michigan Geographic Framework geo-references.
